summary.intent: Fix PR #600 review comments by adding a dedicated `local_only` field to `KnownProvider` struct and renaming/clarifying a test.
summary.outcome: Added `local_only` field to all `KnownProvider` entries, updated `is_local_only()` to use the new field, fixed a test name/comment, committed and pushed. Compilation and tests passed cleanly.

repo: The `KnownProvider` struct in crates/provider-setup/src/lib.rs previously used `key_optional: true` as a proxy for local-only providers (ollama, lmstudio), which was semantically incorrect.
The `local-llm` provider uses `AuthType::Local` which already triggered `is_local_only()` via a separate code path, independent of `key_optional`.
KnownProvider is JSON-serialized with a `keyOptional` field exposed to the frontend â€” this is intentionally separate from `local_only` and was left unchanged.
Only ollama (line ~894) and lmstudio (line ~903) had `key_optional: true` among all known providers.

code.finding: ollama and lmstudio are the only providers with `key_optional: true`; they are also the providers that needed `local_only: true`.
A KnownProvider struct literal in tests also needed the new `local_only` field added to compile.

workflow: Using sed to batch-add a field after a pattern across many struct entries is more efficient than individual Edit calls when the pattern is consistent.
Rust struct literals are exhaustive â€” adding a new field causes compile errors in all construction sites, making it easy to find all places needing updates.

friction: Initially attempted to use Edit tool for each provider entry individually before switching to sed for batch edits â€” wasted several tool calls.
Had to manually check the `local-llm` provider separately because it used `AuthType::Local` rather than `key_optional` to signal local-only behavior, making it easy to miss.
open_items: The `keyOptional` JSON field exposed to the frontend is still derived from `key_optional` rather than `local_only` â€” if these concepts diverge further, the frontend serialization may need revisiting.
